titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/218

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=164 status=live modified=2017-02-26 02:19:53.689294+00 | translation moa=165 status=live modified=2017-02-26 02:19:56.223138+00

p.cl:
Dauid . Rex Scot’ Episcopo Sancti Andree . ⁊ vic’ . ⁊ Omnibus probis
hominibus suis totius Striuelinschire . salutem .

Sciatis quod uolo
⁊ firmiter precipio . quatinus abbas . Sancti Crucis de Edeneb’ habeat
omnes consuetudines illas . in Herez . uidelicet . in aquis . ⁊ in
aliis locis . ⁊ omnes rectitudines que ad ecclesiam eiusdem uille pertinent
sicut melius habuit die illa qua illud habui . in meo dominio .

Testibus Iohanne
episcopo . Roberto de Burnouille’ . Rogero Nepote Roberti episcopi . apud .
Striuelin

div.p: David by the grace of God king of Scots to the Bishop of St Andrews and the sheriff and all worthy men of the whole of the shire of Stirling, Greeting: Know that I will and firmly instruct that the abbot of Holyrood in Edinburgh shall have all those customs in Airth, that is, in waters and other places, and all revenues that belong to the church of the same toun just as it well had on the day that I had it in my demesne. As witnesses, John the Bishop, Robert de Burneville, Roger the bishop’s nephew. At Stirling.
